changesets.summary:
evals: load trace via `chatSessionId` when the legacy `blob` is absent.

After the evalâ†’chatSessions unification dropped the legacy blob writer, new iterations are created with `chatSessionId` set and `blob` unset. `useEvalTraceBlob`'s pre-roundtrip guard still required `iteration.blob`, so the backend action was never called and the trace surface rendered "No trace data is available for this run" while tokens/tool counts populated normally. The hook now gates on either source and re-fires when `chatSessionId` resolves.

eval reporter: send {hostConfig, hostConfigHash} on /sdk/v1/evals/* when backend advertises `evalsHostConfig` (Stage 5 Step 3).

Reporter probes `GET /sdk/v1/info` lazily (cached per baseUrl, fail-safe to "no capability"). When backend supports it AND iteration host snapshots are homogeneous, sends a normalized + content-hashed host config alongside results. Source order: `iteration.hostSnapshot` â†’ `executor.getHostSnapshot?.()` â†’ `MCPJamReportingConfig.host`. Heterogeneous per-iteration snapshots omit the run-level field (per-iteration wire support is a later stage). Old backends are unaffected.
